DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Andrew H. Hall of Akerman Senterfitt (303-260-7712) appearing for defendant Recontrust Company. moving party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative rulingh as follow: DEFENDANTs RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A., THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON and MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustain without leave to amend as to second cause of action, wrongful attempt to sell; sustain with leave to amend as to first cause of action fraud to allege standing, reliance and damages. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, not reported. (501/REQ)

Mtn To Dissolve Prelim Injunction
Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Andrew H. Hall of Akerman Senterfitt (303-260-7712) appearing for defendant Recontrust Company. moving party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follow: DEFENDANTs RECONSTRUCT COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON and MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC.'s Motion To Dissolve Preliminary Injunction. Grant dissolution of preliminary injunction, failed to post bond. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, not reported. (501/REQ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS RECONSTRUCT COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON F/K/A THE BANK OF NEW, MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR PER THE MOVING PARTY. Osc Why Preliminary Injunction Shold Not Issue Enjoining Defendants From Proceeding With A Non-Judicial Sale Of Plaintiff'S Real Property And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stuart Kaufman, plaintiff and moving party in pro per, 415-642-9941. Kristine Poplawski, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3878, for defendant CCSF. Justin D. Balser, Esq., 303-260-7712, appearing by Courtcall, for defendant Recontrust Co., Bank of America.

Counsel appear and the Court adopts its tentative ruling modified as follows: Plaintiff's motion for an order to show cause why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Issue Enjoining Defendants From Proceeding With A Non-Judicial Sale of Plaintiff's Real Property And Temporary Restraining Order is granted. Plaintiff possibility of prevailing. Upon request of counsel for defendant CCSF, Plaintiff Mr. Kaufman has agreed to dismiss CCSF from case. The matter is under submission as to if a bond is required. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay. Not reported.
